definition: A helmet protects a worker’s head on site.
example: Wear your helmet before you enter the work area.

definition: A panel contains switches and electrical control equipment.
example: The electrical panel is in Room A and is ready for inspection.

definition: A switch turns electrical equipment on or off.
example: The main switch should be off before the visual inspection starts.

definition: A drawing shows the size and position of engineering work.
example: The engineer checks the drawing before work starts.

definition: To inspect means to check work or equipment carefully.
example: The supervisor will inspect the electrical panel this morning.

definition: A cable tray is a metal or plastic support system used to route, support, and protect electrical cables along walls, ceilings, and floors.
example: The team installed ten metres of cable tray along the corridor ceiling.

definition: A support holds equipment or containment in the correct position.
example: One cable tray support is missing near Grid B4.

definition: Complete means that all required work is finished.
example: The cable tray work is complete and ready for inspection.

definition: An inspection request is a formal request submitted to the responsible team or consultant to check and verify completed work before proceeding.
example: Please submit the inspection request for Corridor Two before the next work front opens.

definition: A work area is the defined place where a team performs an activity.
example: Keep the electrical work area clean and safe during installation.

definition: A transformer transfers electrical energy between circuits and raises or lowers voltage for safe distribution.
example: The commissioning team checked the transformer oil temperature before energizing the feeder.

definition: Switchgear is a group of breakers, isolators, relays, and panels used to control electrical power safely.
example: The contractor submitted the switchgear test certificates before the client inspection.

definition: A busbar is a rigid conductor that distributes high current within switchboards or distribution boards.
example: Thermal imaging showed a hotspot on the main busbar joint after load testing.

definition: A circuit breaker automatically opens an electrical circuit when it detects overload or fault current.
example: The engineer reset the circuit breaker only after confirming there was no downstream fault.

definition: A relay detects abnormal electrical conditions and sends a signal to trip or control equipment.
example: The protection relay recorded an earth fault during the commissioning test.

definition: Earthing provides a low-resistance path for fault current to protect people and equipment.
example: Make sure the earthing meets the specification.

definition: Insulation resistance indicates how well cable or equipment insulation prevents leakage current.
example: The cable passed the insulation resistance test at one kilovolt.

definition: A short circuit occurs when current flows through an unintended path with very low resistance.
example: The report identified a short circuit between the control cores inside the junction box.

definition: Load flow analysis calculates voltage, current, power, and losses in an electrical network.
example: The consultant requested a revised load flow study after the chiller load increased.

definition: Selectivity ensures the protective device nearest to a fault trips before upstream devices.
example: The protection engineer adjusted the breaker curves to improve selectivity.

definition: Harmonics are unwanted frequency components that distort voltage or current waveforms.
example: The data center added harmonic filters after the UPS caused distortion on the LV board.

definition: Voltage drop is the reduction in voltage between the source and the load caused by conductor impedance.
example: The feeder size was increased because the calculated voltage drop exceeded the specification.

definition: A feeder is a cable or circuit that supplies power from a source to equipment or a distribution board.
example: The site team labelled each feeder before terminating it inside the MCC.

definition: Termination is the prepared connection of a cable core to equipment, terminals, or accessories.
example: QA asked for photos of each cable termination before closing the panel covers.

definition: A conduit protects electrical wiring and provides a defined route through walls or slabs.
example: The architect asked the team to conceal the conduit inside the partition.

definition: Isolation means separating equipment from its energy source before inspection or maintenance.
example: The technician confirmed electrical isolation before opening the motor terminal box.

definition: To energize equipment is to connect it to a live electrical supply.
example: The client allowed the team to energize the panel after all pre-commissioning checks were signed.

definition: A nameplate rating is the official capacity or electrical value printed on equipment by the manufacturer.
example: The inspector compared the installed motor with the nameplate rating in the approved submittal.

definition: A single-line diagram represents a power system using one line for each circuit path.
example: The consultant marked the single-line diagram to show the revised generator connection.

definition: A chiller removes heat from water so it can be circulated through an HVAC cooling system.
example: The commissioning engineer checked the chiller flow rate before starting the performance test.

definition: A pump moves water or another fluid through pipes by creating pressure and flow.
example: The maintenance team aligned the pump before connecting it to the chilled water line.

definition: A valve opens, closes, or regulates the flow of liquid, gas, or air in a system.
example: The supervisor asked for the valve tag number before approving the shutdown.

definition: An actuator converts a control signal into mechanical movement.
example: The BMS contractor replaced the actuator because the damper was not responding.

definition: A damper regulates or stops airflow inside ductwork.
example: If the damper fails the test, corrective action is required.

definition: Ductwork carries supply, return, or exhaust air through a building.
example: The ductwork route was revised to clear the cable tray above the corridor.

definition: Static pressure is the pressure in a duct system that the fan must overcome to move air.
example: The TAB report showed high static pressure across the filter section.

definition: Flow rate is the volume of fluid passing through a point during a specific time period.
example: The consultant rejected the test because the chilled water flow rate was below design.

definition: Pressure drop is the reduction in pressure as fluid or air passes through equipment, pipework, or filters.
example: The engineer calculated the pressure drop across the heat exchanger before selecting the pump.

definition: A heat exchanger transfers heat between two fluids without mixing them.
example: Always verify the heat exchanger before proceeding.

definition: A bearing supports a rotating shaft and reduces friction during operation.
example: The vibration report suggested damage to the fan bearing.

definition: Vibration is repeated movement that can indicate imbalance, misalignment, or mechanical wear.
example: Make sure the vibration meets the specification.

definition: Torque is a rotational force applied to tighten, drive, or rotate a component.
example: The technician used a calibrated wrench to apply the specified torque to the flange bolts.

definition: Alignment ensures shafts, couplings, or components are positioned correctly to avoid stress and wear.
example: The pump alignment record was attached to the mechanical completion dossier.

definition: Balancing reduces uneven forces in rotating equipment or distributes air/water flow to design values.
example: The fan required balancing after the vibration test failed.

definition: A flange is a flat rim that allows pipes, valves, and equipment to be bolted together.
example: The QA inspector found a damaged gasket between the two flanges.

definition: A gasket is a sealing material placed between flanges or joints to prevent leakage.
example: The pressure test failed because the gasket was not seated correctly.

definition: Calibration compares and adjusts instruments so their readings match an accepted reference.
example: If the calibration fails the test, corrective action is required.

definition: Thermal expansion is the increase in length or volume of a material as temperature rises.
example: The pipe supports were revised to allow thermal expansion during operation.

definition: Preventive maintenance is scheduled work carried out to reduce the chance of equipment failure.
example: The facilities team added the pumps to the preventive maintenance plan.

definition: Rebar is steel reinforcement placed inside concrete to improve tensile strength.
example: The inspector checked the rebar spacing before allowing the concrete pour.

definition: Formwork is a temporary structure that holds fresh concrete until it gains strength.
example: The formwork was inspected before the slab was poured.

definition: A footing transfers building loads from columns or walls into the ground.
example: The footing depth was increased after the soil report identified weaker material.

definition: A slab is a flat horizontal concrete element used for floors, roofs, or foundations.
example: The MEP team marked sleeve locations before casting the slab.

definition: A column carries loads from beams or slabs down to foundations.
example: The structural engineer asked for a survey check on the column position.

definition: A beam supports loads and transfers them to columns, walls, or other supports.
example: The contractor could not core through the beam without structural approval.

definition: A shear wall is a structural wall designed to resist wind or seismic lateral loads.
example: The architect coordinated door openings around the shear wall locations.

definition: Settlement is the downward movement of soil or a structure due to loading or ground changes.
example: The monitoring report showed minor settlement near the excavation boundary.

definition: Compaction increases soil density to improve bearing capacity and reduce future settlement.
example: When the compaction is checked, the inspector should document the findings.

definition: Curing maintains moisture and temperature so concrete can develop strength properly.
example: The site team covered the slab for curing immediately after finishing.

definition: Aggregate is sand, gravel, or crushed stone used in concrete or road construction.
example: The concrete mix was rejected because the aggregate grading did not match the approved design.

definition: Bearing capacity is the maximum pressure soil can safely support from a foundation.
example: The geotechnical report confirmed adequate bearing capacity for shallow footings.

definition: A retaining wall supports soil at different levels and prevents collapse or movement.
example: The retaining wall drainage detail was revised after the design review.

definition: A punch list records remaining defects or incomplete items before handover.
example: The client refused handover until the punch list items were closed.

definition: A method statement describes the sequence, resources, risks, and controls for a construction activity.
example: The consultant approved the method statement before the lifting operation.

definition: A shop drawing shows how approved design information will be fabricated, assembled, or installed.
example: The site team could not proceed because the shop drawing was still under review.

definition: An as-built drawing records the actual installed arrangement after construction changes.
example: The handover dossier included updated as-built drawings for all containment routes.

definition: A site instruction directs a contractor to carry out, change, or correct work.
example: The contractor requested a cost assessment after receiving the site instruction.

definition: A workfront is an area or scope where a crew can start or continue work.
example: The planner reported that the electrical team had no clear workfront on level three.

definition: An access constraint is a limitation that prevents people, materials, or equipment from reaching a work area.
example: The crane delivery was delayed due to an access constraint at the service road.

definition: A facade is the visible external face or envelope of a building.
example: The facade contractor coordinated bracket locations with the structural engineer.

definition: An atrium is a tall open space inside a building, often connecting several floors.
example: The smoke control strategy was reviewed because the atrium connects five levels.

definition: A partition divides interior spaces without usually carrying structural loads.
example: The electrical sockets were relocated after the partition layout changed.

definition: Egress is the route people use to leave a building safely during normal use or an emergency.
example: Has the emergency egress been inspected during the safety walkdown?

definition: The building envelope includes walls, roof, glazing, and insulation that protect interior spaces.
example: The design review focused on air leakage through the building envelope.

definition: A louver is a set of angled slats that allows airflow while limiting rain, views, or debris.
example: The generator room louver size was increased to meet ventilation requirements.

definition: A ceiling void is the concealed space above ceiling panels where services often run.
example: The ceiling void was too shallow for the duct and cable tray crossing.

definition: A finish schedule lists floor, wall, ceiling, and other surface finishes for each space.
example: Procurement used the finish schedule to confirm tile quantities.

definition: A baseline programme is the approved project schedule used to measure progress and delays.
example: The planner compared actual progress against the baseline programme in the weekly report.

definition: A milestone is a key date or deliverable used to track project progress.
example: Factory acceptance testing became a critical milestone for the switchgear package.

definition: The critical path is the longest sequence of dependent activities that determines the project completion date.
example: The delay to transformer delivery affected the critical path.

definition: Float is available time before an activity delay affects a milestone or completion date.
example: The planner warned that the cable pulling activity had no remaining float.

definition: A risk register records risks, owners, probability, impact, and mitigation actions.
example: The project manager added late authority approval to the risk register.

definition: A variation order formally changes the contract scope, value, or time.
example: The contractor submitted a variation order for the additional cable route.

definition: A procurement schedule tracks submittals, approvals, manufacturing, delivery, and site need dates.
example: The procurement schedule showed that the pumps would arrive after the planned installation date.

definition: A stakeholder is anyone with an interest in or influence over a project.
example: The project team mapped stakeholders before changing the road closure plan.

definition: A permit to work authorizes hazardous or controlled activities after risks and controls are reviewed.
example: The supervisor refused to start hot work without a valid permit to work.

definition: A toolbox talk is a short site briefing about hazards, controls, and safe work methods.
example: The safety officer gave a toolbox talk before the confined space entry.

definition: Lockout-tagout is a safety procedure that isolates energy and identifies who controls the lock.
example: The technician applied lockout-tagout before servicing the pump starter.

definition: A hazard is anything that can cause injury, illness, damage, or loss.
example: Loose materials near the stairwell were reported as a trip hazard.

definition: A risk assessment identifies hazards, estimates risk, and defines control measures.
example: The lifting plan was rejected because the risk assessment did not cover wind conditions.

definition: A near miss is an unplanned event that did not cause injury but had the potential to do so.
example: The falling bolt was recorded as a near miss and discussed in the safety meeting.

definition: PPE is equipment worn to reduce exposure to workplace hazards.
example: Visitors were not allowed on site without the required PPE.

definition: A confined space is an enclosed or partly enclosed area with limited access and potential atmospheric hazards.
example: The tank was treated as a confined space and required gas testing before entry.

definition: Pre-commissioning includes inspections and static checks completed before systems are operated.
example: The team completed pre-commissioning checks before starting the chilled water pumps.

definition: A functional test verifies that equipment or a system operates as designed.
example: The fire alarm functional test confirmed that the AHU shut down correctly.

definition: An integrated systems test verifies interfaces between systems during a realistic scenario.
example: The integrated systems test checked fire alarm, lifts, smoke fans, and access control together.

definition: A witness test is performed while an authorized party observes and accepts the result.
example: The consultant attended the witness test for the generator load bank.

definition: Snagging is the process of identifying incomplete or defective work before acceptance.
example: The commissioning manager scheduled snagging after the final room inspection.

definition: A handover dossier contains approved records, test results, manuals, certificates, and as-built information.
example: The client would not accept the plantroom without a complete handover dossier.

definition: An O&M manual explains how to operate, maintain, and troubleshoot installed equipment or systems.
example: Facilities asked for the O&M manual before taking over the booster pump set.

definition: Seasonal commissioning verifies system performance under conditions not available at initial handover.
example: The cooling system required seasonal commissioning during peak summer load.

definition: A factory acceptance test verifies equipment at the factory before shipment to site.
example: The client attended the factory acceptance test for the LV switchboard.

definition: A site acceptance test verifies equipment after installation at the project site.
example: The UPS passed the site acceptance test after battery autonomy was confirmed.

definition: A pressure test verifies that pipework or vessels can hold pressure without leakage.
example: The chilled water pipe failed the pressure test at the valve flange.

definition: A load bank test applies controlled load to a generator or UPS to verify performance.
example: The generator completed a four-hour load bank test at eighty percent load.

definition: A continuity test checks whether a conductor or circuit path is complete.
example: The technician performed a continuity test on each control cable core.

definition: A calibrated instrument has been checked against a standard and is suitable for reliable measurement.
example: QA rejected the reading because the pressure gauge calibration certificate had expired.

definition: A test certificate records that a test was completed and states the accepted result.
example: The handover file was missing the fire pump test certificate.

definition: Acceptance criteria define the conditions that must be met for a test or deliverable to be accepted.
example: The team agreed acceptance criteria before starting the integrated systems test.

definition: A rack is a cabinet or frame used to mount servers, switches, and patch panels.
example: The network team reserved two racks for the new storage equipment.

definition: A hot aisle is the row space where server racks discharge warm exhaust air.
example: Containment panels were added above the hot aisle to improve cooling efficiency.

definition: A cold aisle is the row space where servers receive chilled supply air.
example: The operator found blocked floor grilles in the cold aisle during inspection.

definition: PUE measures total facility energy divided by IT equipment energy.
example: The client asked for a lower PUE target during the sustainability review.

definition: Redundancy provides backup equipment or paths so service continues after a failure.
example: The design provides N+1 redundancy for the cooling plant.

definition: A UPS provides temporary power when the main electrical supply fails or fluctuates.
example: The UPS alarm was investigated before the planned shutdown.

definition: A CRAC unit controls temperature and airflow in data center spaces.
example: The CRAC unit filter alarm appeared during the weekly facilities check.

definition: Structured cabling is a standardized cabling system for data, voice, and network services.
example: The installer tested every structured cabling link before handover.

definition: To clarify means to make a point, instruction, or requirement easier to understand.
example: The engineer asked the consultant to clarify the comment before revising the drawing.

definition: To confirm means to verify or formally state that information is correct.
example: Please confirm whether the inspection can proceed tomorrow morning.

definition: To mitigate means to reduce risk, delay, damage, or negative impact.
example: The team proposed night shifts to mitigate the schedule delay.

definition: To escalate means to bring an issue to senior people because it cannot be solved at the current level.
example: The site manager escalated the access issue to the client representative.

definition: A deviation is a departure from a specification, drawing, method, or approved requirement.
example: The QA report recorded a deviation from the approved cable tray route.

definition: A constraint is a condition that limits what can be done, when, or how.
example: The low ceiling height became a major coordination constraint.

definition: A deliverable is a document, drawing, system, or result that must be provided under a project agreement.
example: The commissioning plan was listed as a key deliverable for this month.

definition: An action owner is the person assigned to complete a task after a meeting or review.
example: The minutes named the electrical lead as the action owner for the cable schedule.

definition: Minutes of meeting record decisions, actions, owners, and important discussion points.
example: The project manager circulated the minutes of meeting within twenty-four hours.

definition: An agenda is a planned list of topics to be discussed in a meeting.
example: The chair added commissioning access to the agenda for the coordination meeting.

definition: Consensus is a shared agreement reached by most or all stakeholders after discussion.
example: The design team reached consensus on the revised plantroom layout.

definition: A load schedule lists equipment loads, demand factors, circuit references, and connected capacity.
example: The electrical engineer updated the load schedule after the tenant added kitchen equipment.

definition: A diversity factor accounts for the fact that not all connected loads operate at full demand at the same time.
example: The consultant challenged the diversity factor used for the residential distribution board.

definition: Fault current is the abnormal current that flows when a short circuit or earth fault occurs.
example: Make sure the fault current meets the specification.

definition: An arc flash is a high-energy electrical explosion caused by current passing through air.
example: The safety briefing explained arc flash boundaries before panel testing.

definition: A residual current device trips when leakage current suggests a shock or earth fault risk.
example: The RCD was tested at the temporary site distribution board.

definition: Surge protection limits transient overvoltage caused by lightning or switching events.
example: The data rack panel included surge protection for sensitive network equipment.

definition: Power factor indicates how effectively electrical power is converted into useful work.
example: The utility required power factor correction for the large motor loads.

definition: A capacitor bank supplies reactive power to improve power factor and reduce electrical losses.
example: The capacitor bank was isolated after the protection relay alarm.

definition: A motor control center contains starters, protection, and controls for several motors.
example: If the motor control center fails the test, corrective action is required.

definition: A variable frequency drive controls motor speed by adjusting electrical frequency and voltage.
example: The VFD parameters were adjusted to reduce pump start-up pressure surges.

definition: An air handling unit filters, heats, cools, or moves air through an HVAC system.
example: The AHU access door was blocked by temporary materials during inspection.

meaning: terminal HVAC cooling or heating unit
definition: A fan coil unit uses a fan and coil to condition air in a room or zone.
example: The tenant complained that the fan coil unit above the office was noisy.

definition: An expansion vessel absorbs pressure changes caused by thermal expansion in a closed water system.
example: The pressure fluctuated because the expansion vessel had lost its charge.

definition: A strainer removes debris from water or fluid before it reaches sensitive equipment.
example: The commissioning team cleaned the strainer after flushing the chilled water loop.

definition: Flushing removes debris, scale, and contaminants from pipework before operation.
example: The consultant requested flushing records before approving pump start-up.

definition: Differential pressure is the pressure difference measured across equipment, filters, or pipe sections.
example: The dirty filter alarm was triggered by high differential pressure.

definition: A condensate drain carries water produced by cooling coils or air conditioning equipment.
example: The ceiling leak came from a blocked condensate drain above the meeting room.

definition: Heat load is the thermal demand used to size cooling or heating equipment.
example: Review the heat load before final approval.

definition: Fresh air rate is the quantity of outdoor air provided for ventilation and indoor air quality.
example: The inspector checked the fresh air rate against the occupancy schedule.

definition: Acoustic lining reduces noise transmission in ducts, plantrooms, or enclosures.
example: The consultant requested acoustic lining near the generator exhaust path.

definition: A pile foundation uses long structural elements to transfer loads to deeper, stronger ground.
example: The structural design changed to a pile foundation after weak soil was found.

definition: A pile cap connects piles and distributes column or wall loads into them.
example: The rebar congestion in the pile cap required a revised pouring sequence.

definition: A waterproofing membrane is a protective layer that stops water entering a structure.
example: The basement waterproofing membrane was inspected before backfilling.

definition: An expansion joint allows parts of a structure to move without cracking or damage.
example: The expansion joint cover was damaged near the entrance lobby.

definition: Deflection is the movement or bending of a structural element under load.
example: The beam deflection was within the allowable serviceability limit.

definition: Camber is a slight upward curve built into a member to offset expected deflection.
example: The steel beam was fabricated with camber as shown on the shop drawing.

definition: A screed is a floor layer used to create a smooth, level surface before finishes.
example: The tiling could not start because the screed had not cured.

definition: Blockwork is wall construction using concrete, masonry, or lightweight blocks.
example: The electrical boxes were installed before the blockwork was plastered.

definition: Plastering creates a smooth surface on walls or ceilings before painting or finishing.
example: The plastering crew repaired the wall after the conduit change.

definition: Grouting fills joints, voids, or spaces around tiles, bases, anchors, or equipment.
example: The pump base grouting was completed before final alignment.

definition: Mobilization is the process of bringing labor, equipment, materials, and facilities to site.
example: The contractor planned mobilization immediately after receiving the letter of award.

definition: Demobilization is the removal of temporary facilities, equipment, and teams after completion.
example: Demobilization was delayed because several punch list items remained open.

definition: A material submittal provides product data, samples, and compliance information for approval.
example: The consultant rejected the material submittal because the cable tray coating was not specified.

definition: A mock-up is a sample of work built to confirm appearance, quality, and coordination before full installation.
example: The client approved the hotel room mock-up after reviewing finishes and lighting.

definition: A setback is the required distance between a building element and a property line, road, or other limit.
example: The balcony design was revised to comply with the setback requirement.

definition: Floor area ratio measures total building floor area compared with the site area.
example: The developer reduced one floor to comply with the permitted floor area ratio.

definition: Accessibility means buildings can be used safely and comfortably by people with different mobility or sensory needs.
example: The ramp gradient was changed to improve accessibility at the main entrance.

definition: Wayfinding uses signs, layouts, landmarks, and visual cues to help people find routes.
example: The hospital lobby design improved wayfinding for visitors.

definition: A specification states required materials, workmanship, standards, and performance.
example: The contractor quoted an alternative pump that did not meet the specification.

definition: A scope gap occurs when required work is not clearly included in any party’s scope.
example: The interface meeting identified a scope gap between the facade and steel contractors.

meaning: table showing responsibilities between systems or parties
definition: An interface matrix defines who is responsible for each connection, boundary, or dependency.
example: The interface matrix clarified who would provide power to the BMS panels.

definition: A lookahead schedule shows planned activities for the next few weeks in practical site detail.
example: The foreman used the three-week lookahead schedule to request materials.

definition: Earned value compares planned work, actual cost, and completed work to assess project performance.
example: The controls report used earned value to show schedule slippage.

definition: A cash flow forecast estimates project payments and expenditures over time.
example: Finance requested a revised cash flow forecast after the procurement delay.

definition: A lost time injury is a work-related injury that prevents a person from returning to work for a defined period.
example: The monthly safety report recorded zero lost time injuries.

definition: A method of rescue explains how workers will be safely recovered if an emergency occurs.
example: When the method of rescue is checked, the inspector should document the findings.

definition: An exclusion zone keeps people away from hazards such as lifting, demolition, or energized work.
example: The lifting supervisor set up an exclusion zone around the crane radius.

meaning: lifting or moving loads by hand
definition: Manual handling involves carrying, lifting, pushing, or pulling loads using physical effort.
example: The toolbox talk covered manual handling techniques for moving cable drums.

definition: A scaffold tag indicates whether a scaffold is safe to use after inspection.
example: Workers were told not to access the platform because the scaffold tag was missing.

definition: Hot work includes welding, grinding, cutting, or other activities that can start a fire.
example: The permit required a fire watch for hot work near stored insulation.

definition: A commissioning script sets out steps, expected responses, roles, and acceptance criteria for a test.
example: Does the commissioning script meet the acceptance criteria?

definition: System readiness means equipment, documentation, access, and prerequisites are complete enough for commissioning.
example: The meeting focused on system readiness for the fire alarm cause-and-effect test.

definition: A cause and effect matrix defines how systems should respond to alarms, signals, or events.
example: The fire alarm cause and effect matrix showed that smoke fans should start on detector activation.

definition: The defect liability period is the post-completion contract period during which the contractor must repair defects at their own cost.
example: The leaking valve was reported during the defect liability period.

definition: A commissioning certificate records that a system or item has been tested and accepted.
example: The consultant signed the commissioning certificate for the booster pump set.

definition: A performance test checks capacity, efficiency, flow, pressure, or other required operating values.
example: The chiller performance test was repeated after sensor calibration.

definition: An integrity test verifies that a system, seal, cable, or enclosure remains intact.
example: The containment integrity test confirmed there were no leaks in the clean agent pipework.

definition: A loop test verifies wiring, signal transmission, and response between an instrument and control system.
example: The BMS contractor completed a loop test for each temperature sensor.

definition: A megger test measures insulation resistance in cables or equipment.
example: The cable could not be terminated until the megger test passed.

definition: A torque test checks whether bolts or terminals are tightened to the specified value.
example: The QA checklist required a torque test on all busbar bolts.

definition: Failover is the automatic transfer to backup equipment or paths when the primary system fails.
example: The data center performed a failover test from utility power to generator supply.

definition: Latency is the time delay between a request and a response in a digital system.
example: The operations team investigated high latency between the application and storage network.

definition: A patch panel provides ports where network cables are terminated and cross-connected.
example: The technician traced the server connection back to the patch panel.

meaning: main fiber cabling path between areas
definition: A fiber backbone is the primary fiber-optic cabling linking rooms, floors, or network zones.
example: The fiber backbone was tested before connecting the new data hall.

definition: A cross-connect links circuits, equipment, or patching fields in a controlled cabling environment.
example: The carrier requested a cross-connect from the meet-me room to the client rack.

definition: White space is the controlled data center area where racks, servers, and network equipment are installed.
example: The cooling assessment covered all white space zones in the new data hall.

meaning: room where carriers and customers interconnect
definition: A meet-me room is a controlled space where telecom carriers and customers connect networks.
example: The fiber provider handed over the circuit in the meet-me room.

definition: Uptime is the period when a system or facility is available and functioning.
example: The service agreement required very high uptime for the data center cooling system.

definition: A service level agreement defines expected service performance, response times, and responsibilities.
example: The facilities team reviewed the service level agreement after repeated response delays.

definition: A nonconformance report records work, material, or process that does not meet approved requirements.
example: QA issued a nonconformance report for the incorrect cable gland material.

definition: Corrective action fixes the root cause of a nonconformance or recurring issue.
example: The corrective action required retraining the team on cable termination procedure.

definition: A root cause is the fundamental reason a failure, delay, or defect occurred.
example: The investigation found the root cause was an outdated drawing issued to site.

definition: Lessons learned are practical insights recorded to improve future projects or activities.
example: The project team documented lessons learned after the commissioning delay.

definition: Commercial impact is the cost, payment, claim, or contract effect of an event or decision.
example: The delay notice explained the commercial impact of late design approval.

meaning: formal request or answer about technical detail
definition: A technical clarification resolves uncertainty about drawings, specifications, scope, or design intent.
example: The contractor raised a technical clarification about the fire alarm interface.

definition: A coordination clash occurs when two elements need the same space or interfere with each other.
example: The BIM review found a coordination clash between the duct and cable ladder.

definition: A priority action is an agreed task that needs urgent or early attention.
example: The meeting chair marked the generator fuel issue as a priority action.

definition: An open item is a task, comment, defect, or question that remains unresolved.
example: The consultant asked for an update on all open items before the progress meeting.

definition: A decision log records decisions, dates, owners, and the reasons behind them.
example: The design manager updated the decision log after the client approved the facade color.

definition: An action tracker records tasks, owners, deadlines, and closure status.
example: The weekly action tracker showed that three submittals were overdue.
